# Figure description: Figure 5.png

**Generated by**: claude-sonnet-4-6

---

## Figure Type
Geological/geochemical exploration map combining satellite imagery base with overlaid soil geochemistry data, historical workings, drill hole locations, aeromagnetic structural interpretations, and tenure boundaries. This is a summary exploration map typical of WAMEX report submissions.

---

## Coverage and Subject
The map covers the **Maralinga Downs area**, located within the **Murchison region of Western Australia**, under the tenure of **Fairbairn Goldfields**. The map is titled *"Fairbairn Goldfields – Maralinga Downs – Summary of Exploration."* The coordinate grid spans approximately **585,500mE to 595,000mE** (Easting) and **6,969,500mN to 6,973,500mN** (Northing), suggesting GDA94 / MGA Zone 50 projection. The area covered is roughly 9.5 km (E–W) by 4.5 km (N–S), with a scale bar showing 500m.

---

## Key Content

### Soil Geochemistry (Historical Soils Au ppb)
Soil gold anomalism is plotted as coloured dots:
- **Blue** = 0–10 ppb Au (dominant, widespread)
- **Green** = 10–20 ppb Au
- **Yellow** = 20–40 ppb Au
- **Red** = 40–7,900 ppb Au (highest anomalism)

Red and yellow dots are concentrated in two main zones:
1. A **northern zone** along the top of the map (near coordinates ~6,973,000mN), where a prominent E–W trending string of anomalous soils is present, including several red dots. Two **cyan-coloured swirling contours** (20 ppm Au soil contours) delineate discrete high-priority anomalies in this northern belt.
2. A **southern/central zone** associated with the historical workings corridor trending NNW–SSE through the middle of the map.

### Aeromagnetic Structure
Two **dashed black lines** trending approximately NNW–SSE represent interpreted **mineralised aeromagnetic structures**. These are the dominant structural fabric and host the majority of historical workings and drill intersections. Annotations along these structures note:
- *"No drilling north of Venice"*
- *"No drilling along this structure"*
- *"No drilling along this line of workings"*
- *"No drilling along structure in VG tenure"*

These annotations highlight significant **untested exploration targets** along strike.

### Drill Holes and Gold Intersections
Several **RC drill hole** results are labelled (in red text with white boxes), all located in the southern-central portion of the map:

| Hole ID | Result |
|---|---|
| CJRC208 | 4m @ 9.05 g/t Au |
| CJRC203 | 3m @ 1.41 g/t Au |
| CJRC201 | 1m @ 2.67 g/t Au |
| CJRC305 | 4m @ 1.31 g/t Au |
| CJRC291 | 4m @ 2.3 g/t Au |
| CJRC357 | 4m @ 2.98 g/t Au |
| CJRC205 | 1m @ 2.82 g/t Au |

Additionally, two **RAB/AC drill results** are shown in the far northern portion of the map:
| Hole ID | Result |
|---|---|
| MBAC005 | 3m @ 2.5 g/t Au |
| MBAC236 | 2m @ 8.8 g/t Au |

### Historical Workings
**Magenta/pink dots** represent numerous historical workings scattered throughout the map. Named historical workings/prospects include (from north to south):
- **Warribrook**
- **Retreat, Saltire**
- **Maralinga Downs, Misery**
- **Lady Nitouche, Alliance**
- **Hammerdown**
- **Hidden Treasure Extended**
- **Gem, Highland Mary**
- **Little Sparkler**
- **Venice**
- **Treasure, Little Gem**
- **Hidden Treasure South**
- **Golden Leaf** (southernmost)

### Tenure
**Yellow lines** delineate Fairbairn Goldfields (VG) tenure boundaries, which cover a substantial portion of the mapped area.

## Research Value
This map synthesises all key exploration datasets for the Maralinga Downs project — soil geochemistry, historical workings, aeromagnetic structural trends, and drill results — providing a clear picture of where gold mineralisation has been confirmed and, critically, where significant **along-strike exploration gaps** remain untested. The best drill intersection (CJRC208: 4m @ 9.05 g/t Au) and the high-grade northern RAB result (MBAC236: 2m @ 8.8 g/t Au) demonstrate that **multiple NNW-trending aeromagnetic corridors are genuinely auriferous**, yet most of the strike length within VG tenure is undrilled. A research assistant would conclude this area represents a high-priority target
